design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources (gold, wood, food, stone, iron, coal) → Build economic infrastructure and military units → Manage army formations and morale → Execute tactical battles while avoiding famine and unit mutiny
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Economic pressure (resource depletion causes famine, mutiny, and loss of firepower) balanced against military expansion and scale; managing unlimited unit counts without traditional unit caps
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command massive historical armies across 17th-18th century European warfare without traditional unit limits; master complex formation tactics and diverse unit roles; learn history through comprehensive in-game encyclopedia
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejection of unit caps that defined contemporary RTS games (Age of Empires, Empire Earth); emphasis on economic fragility as core challenge (famine kills units, lack of gold causes mutiny, ammunition depletion silences artillery); formation-based tactical depth where unit groupings (15, 36, 72, 120, 196) require officers/drummers and unlock distinct attack formations

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players develop varied strategies for army control (formations vs. solo units); morale mechanics and defensive bonuses provide tactical depth; resource scarcity creates escalating difficulty as unit counts grow

progression.upgrade_system: Weaponry and defensive stat upgrades researched at barracks, stables, or armories; extended artillery range, accuracy, and build time/cost improvements; tech availability varies by nation
progression.unlock_mechanism: Nations unlock distinct unit rosters and architectural styles; multi-barrelled cannons and certain ship types require academy research; historically-restricted nations limited to basic ship hierarchies
replayability_hooks: Sixteen distinct playable nations with unique tech trees and unit aesthetics
Historical mission scenarios from Thirty Years' War to War of Austrian Succession
Multiplayer strategy development enabling varied tactical approaches

world_building_techniques: In-game encyclopedia providing contextual historical information
Mission scenarios drawn from actual historical conflicts

faction_indicators: Sixteen distinct nations with unique unit identities and architectural styles
Peasants can be captured and turned to enemy allegiance
Nation-specific tech restrictions reflecting historical development
danger_signals: Resource depletion triggering cascading failures: famine kills units, gold depletion causes mutiny, ammunition depletion silences artillery
Artillery shrapnel damages both enemy and player units in vicinity
Naval dominance required for shore bombardment and water crossings
environmental_storytelling: Designated resource areas (mines, fishing spots, wood/stone collection zones) reflect historical geography
Building types and unit aesthetics vary by nation and period
Naval unit progression (yacht → galley → frigate → battleship) mirrors historical naval evolution
